title: [Bug]: NATS primary URL accepts unsupported schemes
summary: The NATS configuration model accepts unsupported primary connection URL schemes instead of failing closed during validation.
observed: A configuration can pass validation with a primary NATS URL scheme that the client should not use for broker connections.
expected: Primary NATS URLs must be limited to explicitly supported schemes before the runner attempts to connect.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that configures an unsupported primary NATS URL scheme.
impact: A typo or unsafe scheme can survive validation and fail later in a less clear runtime path.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior changes. The fix fails configuration before any JetStream messages are fetched.
security: Failing closed on connection schemes reduces accidental use of unsupported transports and keeps network behavior reviewable.
acceptance: Unsupported primary NATS URL schemes are rejected during configuration validation.
Supported NATS URL schemes continue to validate.
The error message identifies the NATS URL field.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, NATS connection option tests, configuration tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update configuration and release notes if the accepted NATS URL scheme policy is clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
